role: An App-level modal/side-panel surface that renders the 'Source' verb result — the full human-readable record, operator notes, and provenance for a selected point, kept as a durable sibling outside the V container.
capabilities: Render full-record source panel from a SourceView object
Extract human-readable prose from Territory identity/corpus_content with fallback heuristics
Truncate oversized content to SOURCE_MAX with honest ellipsis
Count and display inbound board references (edges_in) as provenance
Read back operator-authored comment notes from directions
Show loading, error, empty, and success states
Close via Escape key, scrim click, and close button
substance_why: The file implements complete data transformation helpers, all UI states, close interactions, and explicit operator-law rendering rules rather than stubs.
interactions_present: Clicking the scrim closes the panel
Clicking the close button closes the panel
Displays the selected item's label, meaning, and fuller content
Displays the operator's own notes as a list
Displays the inbound reference count when greater than zero
Shows loading and error status messages
interactions_missing: No data fetching or SourceView construction is wired here — it is entirely prop-driven
No focus trap or focus restoration on open/close
References count is not clickable to inspect the referencing things
No edit, add, or delete note actions
No copy, share, or deep-link action for the source record
aria-modal is set to false rather than true for a modal-like surface
performance_risks: contentFrom recursively walks objects on every render without memoization
Large strings are fully processed before truncation at SOURCE_MAX
Window keydown listener is re-registered whenever the onClose reference changes
Long notes lists are rendered without virtualization
reuse_why: Keep the human-text extraction logic, operator-law semantics, and note/reference concepts, but rebuild the panel shell, accessibility, and close behavior for the redesign.
decomposition_hints: Move pure territory presentation helpers (readTerritoryContent, territoryRefCount, territoryNotes) into a shared territory presenter/util module
Extract a generic modal/side-panel shell (scrim, close button, Escape handling, focus trap) from SourcePanel
Keep SourcePanel as the thin composition layer that maps SourceView fields to the generic panel and presenter helpers
